evals.prompt: Create a new standalone OA control called StoreConfigControl. It should have a Save toolbar button. When Save is clicked, show a success message.
evals.expected_output: Class inherits UserControlBase, implements ICommandManager, has GetCommands() returning a Save CommandAction, DoCommand() with a Save case that calls OnDisplayMessage with MessageEventTypes.Success, and Start/Finish _logger.Information logging.

assertions.name: inherits-usercontrolbase
assertions.description: Class inherits from UserControlBase

assertions.name: implements-icommandmanager
assertions.description: Class implements ICommandManager

assertions.name: has-getcommands-and-docommand
assertions.description: Both GetCommands() and DoCommand() methods are present

assertions.name: uses-ondisplaymessage-for-success
assertions.description: OnDisplayMessage() with MessageEventTypes.Success used for the success toast — not Response.Write or a label

assertions.name: has-start-finish-logging
assertions.description: _logger.Information Starting/Finished pattern present on at least one method

evals.expected_output: Flags: hardcoded connection string and direct SqlConnection/SqlCommand instead of CommandFactory+Dapper, hardcoded ckbcustom schema string, Response.Write instead of OnDisplayMessage, missing Start/Finish logging and try/catch.

assertions.name: flags-raw-ado-net
assertions.description: Flags direct SqlConnection/SqlCommand and recommends CommandFactory + Dapper instead

assertions.name: flags-hardcoded-schema
assertions.description: Flags the hardcoded ckbcustom schema string and says to use _customSchema from config

assertions.name: flags-response-write
assertions.description: Flags Response.Write and recommends OnDisplayMessage() instead

assertions.name: flags-missing-logging
assertions.description: Flags missing Start/Finish _logger.Information logging and try/catch

evals.prompt: I have a standalone OA control. I need it to automatically refresh whenever the user selects a planogram in the main OA explorer. How do I wire this up?
evals.expected_output: Wire SubscriptionManager.AddKeyToSubscribe(PublishedKey.DBKey) in the constructor, override OnMessageReceived, check e.Key equals the subscribed key, call base.Refresh().

assertions.name: uses-addkeytosubscribe-dbkey
assertions.description: SubscriptionManager.AddKeyToSubscribe(PublishedKey.DBKey) called in the constructor

assertions.name: overrides-onmessagereceived
assertions.description: OnMessageReceived is overridden to handle the published value

assertions.name: checks-key-equality
assertions.description: e.Key equality check guards which subscription fired — not blindly reacting to any message

assertions.name: calls-base-refresh
assertions.description: base.Refresh() called inside OnMessageReceived to reload the control

evals.prompt: I'm writing a Submit button handler for a long-running planogram copy operation in my OA control. The operation can take a while so I want to use a job queue. How do I handle the difference between a SaaS (BlueYonder-hosted) environment and an on-prem installation?
evals.expected_output: Always insert a job row first (both paths), read DeploymentMode from config to branch. SaaS: call process proc immediately in the same request. OnPrem: insert only, let background service/Automator process it.

assertions.name: reads-deploymentmode-from-config
assertions.description: Reads DeploymentMode (or equivalent) from config rather than hardcoding the mode — e.g. IsSaaS() helper or ConfigurationHelper.GetConfigSetting("DeploymentMode")

assertions.name: always-inserts-job-first
assertions.description: Job row is always inserted into the job table (cx_job or similar) regardless of SaaS vs OnPrem — both paths insert first for tracking/auditing

assertions.name: saas-calls-process-proc-immediately
assertions.description: On the SaaS branch, the process proc is called immediately in the same web request (synchronous path) — not deferred to a service

assertions.name: onprem-defers-to-service
assertions.description: On the OnPrem branch, only the insert happens — processing is deferred to a background service/Automator that polls the job table

evals.prompt: I need to add a multi-select planogram picker to my standalone OA control. When the user clicks a button, it should open the built-in OA planogram explorer with multi-select enabled, and I need to receive the selected planogram keys back in my control.
evals.expected_output: Implement IPopupControlSubscriber, subscribe to PublishedKey.JsDBKeyList (and HierarchyDBKeyList, DBStatusList) in constructor, open with GetPageKey("CKB Multi-select Planogram Explorer"), Receive() splits JsDBKeyList by '#'.

assertions.name: implements-ipopupcontrolsubscriber
assertions.description: Control implements IPopupControlSubscriber (not a custom popup — uses the built-in explorer)

assertions.name: subscribes-to-jsdkeylist-in-constructor
assertions.description: Constructor subscribes to PublishedKey.JsDBKeyList (the multi-select result key)

assertions.name: opens-correct-explorer-name
assertions.description: Popup opened with PageKey from GetPageKey("CKB Multi-select Planogram Explorer") — exact page name used

assertions.name: receive-splits-by-hash
assertions.description: Receive() method splits the returned keys by '#' delimiter (e.g. JsDBKeyList is '#'-delimited, not comma or space)

evals.prompt: I'm adding some jQuery to my OA control's JavaScript file. I wrapped my initialization code in $(document).ready() but it doesn't seem to fire at all. What's the correct way to wire up JavaScript in an OA control?
evals.expected_output: Explain why $(document).ready fails (jQuery loaded after scripts by OA framework), recommend Sys.Application.add_load, warn IIFE also fails, mention ScriptManager.RegisterClientScriptBlock for code-behind injection.

assertions.name: explains-why-ready-fails
assertions.description: Explains that $(document).ready fails because the OA framework loads jQuery after the script files execute, so jQuery isn't available at script parse time

assertions.name: recommends-sys-application-add-load
assertions.description: Recommends Sys.Application.add_load as the correct OA replacement for $(document).ready

assertions.name: warns-iife-also-fails
assertions.description: Mentions that a jQuery IIFE wrapper (e.g. (function($){...})(jQuery)) also fails for the same reason — not just the document.ready form

assertions.name: scriptmanager-for-codebehind
assertions.description: If mentioning code-behind script injection, recommends ScriptManager.RegisterClientScriptBlock rather than Page.ClientScript (which is silently ignored when ScriptManager is present)
